fixed.market_context: AI synthetic user x design thinking x user research SaaS
fixed.product_category: B2B SaaS for AI pre-validation and research scoping
fixed.buyer_type: primary platform user and likely individual buyer

preferred.location: Hong Kong startup or indie builder scene
preferred.company_stage: pre-seed or very early-stage solo venture
preferred.work_context: building alone or with one collaborator while balancing speed, limited budget, and constant uncertainty
preferred.research_maturity: low to moderate; knows research matters but lacks a repeatable process
current_workarounds: uses ChatGPT or general AI for brainstorming
posts rough ideas on small online communities
tries lightweight landing-page or waitlist tests
core_attraction: gets structured pushback before spending too much time building
can test several target users quickly without recruiting real participants first
helps identify what to ask in later human interviews
core_objections: may worry the personas are just polished AI roleplay
may overuse synthetic feedback as a replacement for real market evidence
may resist paying if the output feels too similar to prompting a general LLM directly
questions_to_surface: How is this different from just prompting ChatGPT myself?
What does this help me learn before I spend money on real user interviews?
Where should I stop trusting synthetic feedback and switch to humans?
Can this actually challenge me instead of agreeing with my pitch?
preferred.trust_boundary: needs clear synthetic-only disclaimers, visible reasoning, and evidence that the platform can produce sharper objections than a generic AI chat
preferred.pricing_sensitivity: moderate to high; willing to pay if it saves time and prevents obvious founder mistakes, but suspicious of enterprise-style pricing
preferred.retention_hypothesis: retains only if the tool becomes part of repeated weekly product decisions rather than a one-off novelty experiment
interview_focus: whether the product solves a real pre-validation pain
whether the founder understands the boundary between synthetic and human evidence
what proof would make this worth paying for
